target: data/wins.json WIN-025 detail_evidence — architectural disjoint between detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text (c4 Hole 5 / ISS-1486). Audit finds no patch required: EXP-235 (integrated 2026-04-23T14:33Z under ISS-1463) already added the FSF scaling-equation setup, the FSF = H(r)/r geometric-ratio concession with Pearson r ≈ 0.9999, and the 2017 Boulder absolute-magnitude import to detail_evidence P4. The curmudgeon c4 review (reviewed_at 2026-04-23T13:37Z) was conducted against pre-EXP-235 text; ISS-1486 (created 2026-04-23T16:28Z from c4 H5) is a stale duplicate of c3 H3 that EXP-235 already resolves.

audit.question: Does current WIN-025 detail_evidence introduce the dome scaling equation (δZ = baseline × coverage × FSF), the FSF = H(r)/r geometric ratio, the Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 concession, and the 2017 Boulder absolute-magnitude import, BEFORE detail_verdict_text concedes them?
audit.method: Compared current data/wins.json WIN-025.detail_evidence against EXP-235 patch after-string and key architectural anchors. Verified via node substring checks against the live file (contents as of this run): includes "scaling equation", "FSF = H(r)/r", "Pearson", "2017 Boulder", "baseline × eclipse-coverage × FSF", and the full EXP-235 after-string verbatim.
audit.conclusion: All four architectural elements c4 H5 / ISS-1486 requires are already present in current detail_evidence. The verdict-concession-without-evidence-premise failure mode no longer obtains. No text patch is required.

holes_addressed.hole: ISS-1486 / c4 Hole #5 (moderate) — architectural disjoint between detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text (c3 H3 residual)
holes_addressed.description: c4 H5 said detail_evidence "still describes WIN-025 purely as Chapman relabel" and does not introduce the scaling equation or BOU anchor before detail_verdict_text concedes them. That was true of the text the curmudgeon was looking at (pre-EXP-235, which EXP-229 had not touched P4). EXP-235 (ISS-1463) targeted exactly that paragraph and added the scaling equation, FSF = H(r)/r with Pearson r ≈ 0.9999, and the 2017 Boulder baseline import. EXP-235 was integrated ~55 minutes after the c4 review completed; the curmudgeon could not have seen the integrated text.
holes_addressed.fix: No fix required. EXP-235 already delivered it. Recommended decider action: close ISS-1486 as "already resolved by EXP-235 / ISS-1463." If the decider prefers an independent verification, running the substring checks in this EXP's audit.method field against data/wins.json will confirm all architectural anchors are present.

duplicate_of: ISS-1463 / EXP-235 (same curmudgeon hole — c3 H3 renamed to c4 H5 in the next review cycle, with identical recommendation text and fix)
kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon c4 review is substantively right about the architectural disjoint — the pre-EXP-235 text did have exactly the problem described. What it missed is that the fix had already been written and was queued for integration at the time of review. This is a consequence of our async pipeline: curmudgeon reviews a frozen text snapshot, analyst writes a patch concurrently, decider integrates later. Occasional stale-review findings are the tolerated cost of parallelism.
out_of_scope_deliberately: All other c4 holes (H1 finding, H2 Kp — resolved by EXP-238, H3 detail_verdict_text self-retraction, H4 introduced-specifically-to-repair, H6 tldr_verdict, H7 claim-redundancy, H8 Kim & Chang citation verification). c4 H1 and H3 were already auto-closed by decider run 11 (ISS-1481 and ISS-1483) in the same commit (4386c459) that created ISS-1486.
propagation_note: Zero propagation. No fields touched. Decider should close ISS-1486 and mark EXP-239 resolved on integration.
